body_excerpt:
Greptile Summary

This PR introduces first-class keyspace notification support for StackExchange.Redis, including new `KeyNotification` / `KeyNotificationType` / `KeyNotificationTypeFastHash` types, factory methods on `RedisChannel` for building well-typed keyspace and keyevent subscription channels, and a refactored `Subscription` hierarchy that adds a `MultiNodeSubscription` class for broadcasting subscriptions across all cluster nodes. - **New keyspace API**: `RedisChannel.KeyEvent`, `KeySpacePrefix`, `KeySpacePattern`, `KeySpaceSingleKey` build correctly-formed subscription channels; `KeyNotification` exposes alloc-free key/type access; `ChannelMessage.TryParseKeyNotification` provides a convenient entry point. - **Subscription refactor**: The single `Subscription` class is split into `SingleNodeSubscription` (unchanged semantics) and `MultiNodeSubscription` (subscribes/unsubscribes across all primaries), with `ResultProcessor` and `ServerSelectionStrategy` updated to wire up multi-node tracking. - **Channel prefix handling**: `RawResult.AsRedisChannel` and `PhysicalConnection.Write` are updated so that keyspace channels bypass the user-configured channel prefix, preventing missed notifications when a prefix is active.

Confidence Score: 2/5

Not safe to merge as-is: the inverted condition in MultiNodeSubscription silently destroys active keyspace subscriptions on every reconnect cycle. The new MultiNodeSubscription class is the heart of the keyspace notification feature, and its RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints method removes connected servers rather than disconnected ones. Every reconnection event (network blip, server restart, heartbeat-triggered UpdateSubscriptions) would evict all active server associations, causing the subscription to stop delivering messages without any error signal to the caller. src/StackExchange.Redis/Subscription.cs — the MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints method needs the condition inverted; src/StackExchange.Redis/ConnectionMultiplexer.cs — minor typo in GetServerSnaphotMemory.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | src/StackExchange.Redis/Subscription.cs | New file splitting the old inline Subscription class into abstract base + SingleNodeSubscription + MultiNodeSubscription; MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints has an inverted condition that removes connected servers instead of disconnected ones, breaking multi-node keyspace subscriptions on reconnect. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/KeyNotification.cs | New public ref struct providing alloc-free access to keyspace/keyevent notification fields; parsing, key extraction, and type resolution logic appears correct. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/RedisChannel.cs | Adds MultiNode and IgnoreChannelPrefix flags plus factory methods (KeySpaceSingleKey, KeySpacePattern, KeySpacePrefix, KeyEvent) for building keyspace/keyevent subscription channels; channel-prefix suppression and cluster slot routing logic looks correct. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/ConnectionMultiplexer.cs | Adds GetServerSnapshotMemory (with a typo 'Snaphost') and a ChannelPrefix helper; otherwise minimal, safe changes. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/RedisSubscriber.cs | Subscription creation now dispatches to MultiNodeSubscription vs SingleNodeSubscription; UpdateSubscriptions delegates to RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints (which has the bug in MultiNodeSubscription). | | src/StackExchange.Redis/ResultProcessor.cs | TrackSubscriptionsProcessor now calls AddEndpoint/TryRemoveEndpoint instead of SetCurrentServer, correctly wiring up multi-node subscription tracking on subscribe/unsubscribe acknowledgements. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/ServerSelectionStrategy.cs | HashSlot now honours IgnoreChannelPrefix and uses the key-routing span for cluster slot resolution; new CanServeSlot helper correctly checks primary and replica nodes. | | src/StackExchange.Redis/RawResult.cs | AsRedisChannel now correctly passes through __keyspace@ / __keyevent@ channels even when a channel prefix is configured and doesn't match, enabling keyspace notification delivery regardless of prefix settings. | | tests/StackExchange.Redis.Tests/PubSubKeyNotificationTests.cs | New integration tests covering subscribe/observe flows for keyevent and keyspace channels, both standalone and cluster, with and without channel prefix; good coverage of the new API surface. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant App participant RedisSubscriber participant MultiNodeSubscription participant ServerSnapshot participant ServerEndPoint App->>RedisSubscriber: SubscribeAsync(KeyEvent channel) RedisSubscriber->>MultiNodeSubscription: new MultiNodeSubscription(flags) RedisSubscriber->>MultiNodeSubscription: EnsureSubscribedToServerAsync() MultiNodeSubscription->>ServerSnapshot: GetServerSnapshotMemory() loop each server (non-sentinel, matching replica flag) MultiNodeSubscription->>ServerEndPoint: SUBSCRIBE / UNSUBSCRIBE ServerEndPoint-->>MultiNodeSubscription: ACK → AddEndpoint / TryRemoveEndpoint end Note over MultiNodeSubscription: On reconnect, UpdateSubscriptions() calls RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints() Note over MultiNodeSubscription: BUG: inverted condition removes connected servers App->>RedisSubscriber: Message arrives RedisSubscriber->>MultiNodeSubscription: ForInvoke(channel, value) MultiNodeSubscription-->>App: handler(channel, value) + KeyNotification.TryParse ``` Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["chore: add workflow\_dispatch to CI and C..."](https://github.com/ericcogen/ai-review-eval-stackexchange-redis/commit/18aadb6a4f26ac6bf4fbfda2065df9edf64f793b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33805141) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.
